title: L004: Add network chaos scenarios and CI integration
summary: All 10 items from berendt's external review #2 (1 critical, 7 warnings, 2 infos) were correctly addressed. C-001 (run-chaos label trigger) fixed by adding `types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]` to pull_request trigger. W-005/W-006 (fault injection assertion) fixed by adding AllInjected gate steps. W-007 (duration budget) fixed by increasing partition duration to 300s. I-002 (container index) fixed by switching to name-based jsonpath. Documentation in ci-workflow.md and chaos-e2e-tests.md comprehensively updated for W-002/W-003/W-004. Review patterns captured for W-001 (submodule bumps) and C-001 (label trigger types). Three pre-existing stale paragraphs remain in chaos-e2e-tests.md (dependencies, timeout, trigger table) that were not part of berendt's explicit list but are noted as suggestions.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and follow 6-step chaos pattern
Edge cases covered (AllInjected gate, pod-not-found guard, duration safety net)
Catch blocks present on all assert steps
Test structure consistent with sibling chaos suites

code_quality_checklist.item: Clear comments with CC-0049 and scenario ID references
SPDX headers present on all files
No dead code or unnecessary complexity

security_checklist.item: No path traversal or injection risks

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
NetworkChaos CRD specs match chaos-mesh.org/v1alpha1 schema
CI job configuration matches documented behavior

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Uses shared diagnostics.sh for catch blocks

fail_fast_checklist.item: AllInjected assertion gates degradation check (prevents vacuous passes)
Pod existence guard before restart count check

defensive_checklist.item: Container name-based jsonpath instead of index-based
kubectl wait replaces fixed sleep for injection confirmation

suggested_improvements: docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md:149-151 — Pre-existing: Dependencies paragraph still references e2e-operator ('The job depends on all gate jobs ... and e2e-operator'). ci-workflow.md was correctly updated but this file was not. Consider updating to match: 'The job depends on all gate jobs (lint, shellcheck, test, test-integration, verify-codegen).'

docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md:158-159 — Pre-existing: Timeout paragraph says '60 minutes' but actual is 45 minutes (changed in CC-0049 REQ-006). ci-workflow.md was correctly updated but this file was not.

docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md:144-148 — Pre-existing: Trigger conditions table does not mention the run-chaos label trigger (CC-0049 REQ-007). ci-workflow.md correctly documents both triggers. Consider adding a row: '| Pull request | Path filter matches, Go code changed, **or `run-chaos` label present** |'

docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md:777-789 — Pre-existing: Catch Block Diagnostics table has no columns for Network Partition (006) or Network Latency (007). Both scenarios use diagnostics.sh in their catch blocks and the partition test additionally dumps NetworkChaos CR status.

.planwerk/review_patterns/verify-hardcoded-paths-match-actual-repository-layout.md — The CC-0049 example (label trigger type configuration) is a workflow trigger issue, not a hardcoded path issue. Consider creating a dedicated review pattern (e.g., 'verify-github-actions-trigger-types-for-label-gated-jobs.md') or a more general 'verify-workflow-configuration-matches-feature-requirements.md' pattern.
next_steps: Update the 3 pre-existing stale paragraphs in chaos-e2e-tests.md (dependencies, timeout, trigger table) to match the corrected ci-workflow.md — can be done in this PR or as a follow-up
Add Network Partition (006) and Network Latency (007) columns to the Catch Block Diagnostics table in chaos-e2e-tests.md

detected_patterns.name: NetworkChaos AllInjected gate before effect assertion
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new NetworkChaos-based chaos E2E test that asserts degradation or stability after fault injection
detected_patterns.description: NetworkChaos tests must verify the fault is actually injected (AllInjected=True) before asserting its effects. Unlike PodChaos pod-kill which has immediate visible impact (pod deletion), NetworkChaos CRs can be accepted by the API server without effect if the selector doesn't match target pods. Without the AllInjected gate, a mis-targeted selector causes the degradation assertion (e.g., DatabaseReady=False) to pass vacuously if the condition briefly flickers for an unrelated reason. Two implementation variants exist: (1) a dedicated Chainsaw assert step for degradation-recovery tests where the gate and effect assertion are separate steps, and (2) a kubectl wait inside a script step for no-regression tests where the gate is bundled with stability verification.

examples.code:
- try:
    - assert:
        resource:
          apiVersion: chaos-mesh.org/v1alpha1
          kind: NetworkChaos
          metadata:
            name: partition-mariadb
            namespace: openstack
          status:
            conditions:
            - type: AllInjected
              status: "True"

echo "Waiting for NetworkChaos latency-mariadb injection..."
          kubectl wait networkchaos/latency-mariadb -n openstack \
            --for=condition=AllInjected --timeout=60s
          echo "NetworkChaos latency injection confirmed active"
